description: Surface-mine haul-road design norms from public mining-engineering sources. Used to ground the DES settings the model generator emits (grades, rolling resistance, traction, speed limits). Values are typical/recommended ranges, not site measurements.

max_grade_pct.note: most mine grades fall 6-10%; many regulations cap sustained grade at 10%

rolling_resistance_pct.note: approx +2% per 25 mm tyre penetration above the base

running_surface_width.note: widen on curves for body overhang; add ~0.5x truck width clearance

superelevation_pct.curve_speed_formula: V^2 = 127 * R * (e + f), e=superelevation rate, f=side friction

sources: US Bureau of Mines — Design of Surface Mine Haulage Roads, A Manual (Kaufman & Ault)
Tannant & Regensburg — Guidelines for Mine Haul Road Design (2001)
Caterpillar Performance Handbook (Ed. 29): rolling resistance, coefficients of traction
